div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Steinhardt) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
1060. Department’s 586, April 26, 4 p.m. In response to the note addressed to the Commissariat for Foreign Affairs based on the instruction just cited the Embassy has now received a further communication from the Commissariat reading as follows:
“The People’s Commissariat for Foreign Affairs of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics has the honor to confirm the receipt of note no. 293 dated April 28, 1941, from the Embassy of the United States of America, containing a statement to the effect that on April 14, 1941 the Secretary of State of the United States of America presented to the plenipotentiary representative of the U. S. S. R. in the United
States of America a note containing an explanation of the actions of the postal authorities of the United States of America in destroying certain Soviet publications addressed to the United States of America stating that the Government of the United States of America is not aware of any limitation in regard to subscriptions of Soviet organizations to American publications.
Upon studying the note of April 14, 1941, from the Department of State and the Embassy’s note of April 28, 1941, the Soviet Government once more asserts that it knows for certain that this régime of actual censorship is not applied [by?] authorities of the American Government to the mails of an overwhelming majority of countries and that individual American publishing houses set limits on subscriptions to American publications by organizations or individual citizens of other countries.
The Soviet Government regards the above mentioned measures of the American authorities and of individual American publishing houses as actions of a clearly discriminatory character in respect to the Soviet Union.
In the interests of the maintenance of normal postal communications between the two countries and of the preservation and development of cultural relations between them the Soviet Government insists on its requests that the Government of the United States of America take the necessary measures for the resumption of completely unimpeded deliveries of Soviet postal matter to addresses in the United States of America and for the elimination of the hindrances set up by American publishing houses which affect the subscriptions of Soviet organizations to American publications.”
